categories.description: Bacteria that produce short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) such as butyrate, propionate, and acetate through fermentation of dietary fiber. SCFAs are critical for colonocyte energy, immune regulation, and gut barrier integrity.
categories.description_zh: 通过发酵膳食纤维产生丁酸、丙酸、乙酸等短链脂肪酸（SCFA）的细菌。短链脂肪酸对结肠上皮细胞供能、免疫调节和肠道屏障完整性至关重要。
categories.health_relevance: Low SCFA producers are associated with IBD, colorectal cancer, obesity, and metabolic syndrome.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 短链脂肪酸产生菌减少与炎症性肠病（IBD）、结直肠癌、肥胖和代谢综合征相关。

categories.description: Bacteria with bile salt hydrolase (BSH) or 7α-dehydroxylation activity that transform primary bile acids (cholic acid, chenodeoxycholic acid) into secondary bile acids. These metabolites act as signaling molecules affecting TGR5 and FXR receptors.
categories.description_zh: 具备胆盐水解酶（BSH）或 7α-脱羟基活性的细菌，可将初级胆汁酸（胆酸、鹅去氧胆酸）转化为次级胆汁酸。这些代谢物作为信号分子作用于 TGR5 和 FXR 受体。
categories.health_relevance: Dysregulated bile acid metabolism is linked to cholestatic liver disease, gallstones, colorectal cancer, and T2D.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 胆汁酸代谢紊乱与胆汁淤积性肝病、胆结石、结直肠癌和 2 型糖尿病相关。

categories.description: Bacteria that convert dietary tryptophan into indole derivatives, tryptamine, and serotonin precursors. These metabolites modulate gut motility, host immunity, and the gut-brain axis.
categories.description_zh: 将膳食色氨酸转化为吲哚衍生物、色胺及血清素前体的细菌。这些代谢物调节肠道蠕动、宿主免疫以及脑-肠轴。
categories.health_relevance: Altered tryptophan metabolism is implicated in IBS, depression, autism spectrum disorder, and inflammatory diseases.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 色氨酸代谢改变与肠易激综合征（IBS）、抑郁、孤独症谱系障碍和炎症性疾病相关。

categories.description: Bacteria capable of dissimilatory sulfate reduction, producing H₂S. At high concentrations, H₂S inhibits colonocyte respiration and compromises the epithelial barrier, contributing to IBD pathology.
categories.description_zh: 可进行异化硫酸盐还原并产生硫化氢（H₂S）的细菌。高浓度 H₂S 抑制结肠上皮细胞呼吸、损伤肠道屏障，参与炎症性肠病的发病机制。
categories.health_relevance: Elevated H₂S producers associated with UC, colorectal cancer, and high-fat diet consumption.
categories.health_relevance_zh: H₂S 产生菌升高与溃疡性结肠炎（UC）、结直肠癌及高脂饮食相关。

categories.description: Bacteria producing trimethylamine (TMA) from dietary choline, betaine, or L-carnitine. TMA is hepatically oxidized to TMAO, a proatherogenic metabolite associated with cardiovascular disease risk.
categories.description_zh: 利用膳食胆碱、甜菜碱或 L-肉碱产生三甲胺（TMA）的细菌。TMA 在肝脏内被氧化为 TMAO，是与心血管疾病风险相关的致动脉粥样硬化代谢物。
related_pathways_zh: 肉碱 Rieske 型加氧酶 (CntA/B)
categories.health_relevance: High TMAO levels are predictive of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE), thrombosis, and CKD progression.
categories.health_relevance_zh: TMAO 水平升高可预测主要不良心血管事件（MACE）、血栓形成及慢性肾病（CKD）进展。

categories.name_zh: 内毒素产生菌（革兰氏阴性）
categories.description: Gram-negative bacteria whose outer membrane contains lipopolysaccharide (LPS). Translocation of LPS into systemic circulation ('metabolic endotoxemia') triggers TLR4/NF-κB signaling and chronic low-grade inflammation.
categories.description_zh: 外膜含有脂多糖（LPS）的革兰氏阴性细菌。LPS 进入体循环（即所谓代谢性内毒素血症）可激活 TLR4/NF-κB 通路，诱发慢性低度炎症。
categories.health_relevance: Metabolic endotoxemia linked to obesity, T2D, NAFLD, and systemic inflammatory conditions.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 代谢性内毒素血症与肥胖、2 型糖尿病、非酒精性脂肪肝（NAFLD）及全身性炎症状态相关。

categories.description: Bacteria capable of de novo synthesis of folate (vitamin B9) and cobalamin (vitamin B12), contributing to host B-vitamin status, one-carbon metabolism, and DNA methylation.
categories.description_zh: 可从头合成叶酸（维生素 B9）和钴胺素（维生素 B12）的细菌，参与宿主 B 族维生素状态、一碳代谢和 DNA 甲基化。
categories.health_relevance: Microbial B-vitamin production impacts host epigenetics, neural tube development, and anemia susceptibility.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 微生物 B 族维生素合成影响宿主表观遗传、神经管发育及贫血易感性。

categories.description: Bacteria that produce or modulate neurotransmitters and their precursors, influencing the gut-brain axis through vagal signaling, enteroendocrine cells, and systemic metabolite circulation.
categories.description_zh: 可产生或调控神经递质及其前体的细菌，通过迷走神经信号、肠内分泌细胞和体循环代谢物影响脑-肠轴。
categories.health_relevance: Gut-brain axis disruption linked to depression, anxiety, autism, Parkinson's disease, and Alzheimer's disease.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 脑-肠轴紊乱与抑郁、焦虑、孤独症、帕金森病及阿尔茨海默病相关。

categories.description: Bacteria capable of degrading host-derived mucin glycoproteins in the intestinal mucus layer. Controlled mucin turnover supports niche balance, but excessive degradation compromises the epithelial barrier.
categories.description_zh: 可降解肠道黏液层中宿主黏蛋白糖蛋白的细菌。适度的黏蛋白周转可维持生态位平衡，但过度降解会损伤上皮屏障。
categories.health_relevance: Akkermansia muciniphila supports barrier integrity and metabolic health; excessive mucin degradation linked to IBD and pathogen invasion.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 嗜黏蛋白阿克曼菌（Akkermansia muciniphila）支持屏障完整性和代谢健康；黏蛋白过度降解与炎症性肠病及病原体入侵相关。

categories.description: Bacteria that ferment amino acids (especially aromatic and sulfur-containing amino acids) to produce phenols, indoles, amines, and branched-chain fatty acids. Excessive protein fermentation produces potentially toxic metabolites.
categories.description_zh: 发酵氨基酸（尤其是芳香族和含硫氨基酸）生成酚类、吲哚、胺类和支链脂肪酸的细菌。过量蛋白质发酵可产生潜在毒性代谢物。
key_metabolites_zh: 支链脂肪酸（异丁酸、异戊酸）
categories.health_relevance: Excessive amino acid fermentation linked to CKD uremic toxins (p-cresyl sulfate), hepatic encephalopathy (ammonia), and colon cancer.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 氨基酸过度发酵与慢性肾病尿毒素（对甲酚硫酸盐）、肝性脑病（氨升高）及结肠癌相关。

categories.description: Bacteria involved in hydrogen production (fermenters) and hydrogen consumption (acetogens, sulfate reducers). Methanogenic archaea also consume H₂. This cross-feeding network regulates fermentation thermodynamics and gas production.
categories.description_zh: 参与氢气产生（发酵菌）与消耗（产乙酸菌、硫酸盐还原菌）的细菌；产甲烷古菌亦消耗 H₂。此交叉喂养网络调控发酵热力学和产气量。
categories.health_relevance: H₂ accumulation inhibits fermentation efficiency; methane producers linked to constipation-predominant IBS and slow transit.
categories.health_relevance_zh: H₂ 积累抑制发酵效率；产甲烷菌与便秘型肠易激综合征（IBS-C）及慢传输型便秘相关。

categories.name_zh: 乳酸产生与交叉喂养菌
categories.description: Bacteria producing lactate as a primary fermentation end-product. Lactate serves as a critical cross-feeding substrate for butyrate-producing bacteria, linking primary fermentation to SCFA production.
categories.description_zh: 以乳酸为主要发酵终产物的细菌。乳酸是丁酸产生菌的关键交叉喂养底物，将初级发酵与 SCFA 产生衔接起来。
related_pathways_zh: 乳酸 → 丁酸交叉喂养
categories.health_relevance: Lactate accumulation (D-lactic acidosis) is dangerous in short-bowel syndrome; balanced lactate cross-feeding supports gut health.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 乳酸积累（D-乳酸酸中毒）在短肠综合征中具有危险性；均衡的乳酸交叉喂养则支持肠道健康。

categories.description: Bacteria capable of degrading dietary oxalate, reducing its intestinal absorption and lowering the risk of kidney stone formation. Loss of these bacteria after antibiotic use increases enteric hyperoxaluria.
categories.description_zh: 可降解膳食草酸的细菌，减少其肠道吸收，降低肾结石形成风险。抗生素使用后这些细菌的丢失会增加肠源性高草酸尿症的风险。
related_pathways_zh: 草酰辅酶 A 脱羧酶通路
甲酰辅酶 A 转移酶
categories.health_relevance: Loss of oxalate degraders linked to calcium oxalate kidney stones and enteric hyperoxaluria post-Roux-en-Y gastric bypass.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 草酸降解菌丢失与草酸钙肾结石及 Roux-en-Y 胃旁路术后肠源性高草酸尿症相关。

categories.description: Bacteria encoding extensive glycoside hydrolase (GH) and polysaccharide utilization loci (PUL) repertoires. These keystone species initiate dietary fiber breakdown, releasing oligosaccharides for cross-feeding to other community members.
categories.description_zh: 编码大量糖苷水解酶（GH）和多糖利用位点（PUL）的细菌。这些关键物种启动膳食纤维降解，释放寡糖供其他群落成员交叉喂养。
categories.health_relevance: Low fiber-degrader abundance associated with Westernized diet, reduced microbial diversity, and increased inflammatory disease risk.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 纤维降解菌丰度偏低与西方化饮食、菌群多样性下降及炎症性疾病风险升高相关。

categories.name_zh: 条件致病菌（病生共生体）
categories.description: Normally commensal bacteria that can cause disease under conditions of dysbiosis, immune suppression, or barrier breach. These pathobionts carry virulence factors that are typically suppressed in a healthy, competitive microbial community.
categories.description_zh: 通常作为共生菌存在，但在菌群失调、免疫抑制或屏障破损条件下可致病的细菌。这些条件致病菌携带的毒力因子在健康且竞争平衡的群落中通常被抑制。
categories.health_relevance: Bloom of pathobionts observed in antibiotic-associated diarrhea, VAP, neonatal sepsis, and post-transplant infection.
categories.health_relevance_zh: 条件致病菌扩张见于抗生素相关性腹泻、呼吸机相关肺炎（VAP）、新生儿脓毒症及移植后感染。
